title: No package script exposes a dedicated type or lint quality gate
reasoning: The package scripts provide dev, build, preview, Astro passthrough, and Vitest commands, but no explicit typecheck or lint script. In a TypeScript/Astro project this makes it easy for CI or release automation to run build and tests without a dedicated static analysis gate, allowing type and lint regressions that tests may not exercise.
recommendation: Add project-native quality scripts such as typecheck and lint, then wire them into the release or CI command path if this repository has one.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The listed scripts only run Vitest tests; tests do not establish that static type or lint checks are available or enforced.
minimumFixScope: Update package.json scripts to expose the missing quality gate commands and add any required dev dependencies/configuration.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists: package.json lines 5-12 are still the scripts block, but it now includes "typecheck": "ASTRO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 astro check" and "lint": "npm run typecheck". package.json also now declares @astrojs/check and typescript devDependencies, and package-lock.json contains the corresponding added dependency graph. npm run confirms both scripts are exposed, and a Node assertion verified the expected scripts/dependencies. Running npm run lint showed lint invokes typecheck/astro check, but full execution could not complete in this read-only sandbox because Astro attempted to write .astro/content.d.ts. npm run test was also blocked by the read-only sandbox when Vite tried to write node_modules/.vite-temp. Those failures are sandbox write-permission failures, not evidence that the quality gate scripts are missing. The Clawpatch stored finding still says open, but it is stale relative to the current worktree.
commands: rg -n "fnd_sig-feat-config-7528cb5b98-22bb6_cf0e58344c|No package script exposes" .clawpatch package.json docs tests --glob '!node_modules/**'

node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); if (p.scripts?.typecheck !== 'ASTRO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 astro check') throw new Error('missing expected typecheck'); if (p.scripts?.lint !== 'npm run typecheck') throw new Error('missing expected lint'); if (!p.devDependencies?.['@astrojs/check'] || !p.devDependencies?.typescript) throw new Error('missing typecheck deps'); console.log('quality scripts and deps present');"
